listeners.purpose: Marks every monitoring_provider_config for the suspended tenant as is_active=false + disables every health_check (is_enabled=false + cancels pending RunHealthCheckJob). Does NOT soft-delete — restoration on tenant reactivation is one flip away. Fires MonitoringProviderDeactivated (reason='tenant_suspended') per config + HealthCheckDisabled (reason='tenant_suspended') per check.
Reverses SuspendMonitoringOnTenantSuspended. Only re-activates providers + checks that were disabled BY the suspension cascade (checks the audit trail for reason='tenant_suspended').

Cascade delete via FK CASCADE across all six monitoring tables. This listener writes the compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (health_checks / health_check_runs / monitoring_alerts / monitoring_alert_policies / monitoring_incidents / monitoring_provider_configs purged). No cross-provider deletion requests needed — the providers don't retain tenant-identifiable data (monitoring is system health, not user data).

Reacts to entitlement revocation on any of the 9 monitoring entitlements. Consequences depend on which entitlement was revoked: monitoring_capture revoked → disable every health_check + deactivate every provider_config for the tenant; monitoring_paging revoked → deactivate PagerDuty + Opsgenie provider_configs; monitoring_apm revoked → deactivate Datadog; monitoring_prometheus revoked → deactivate Prometheus; monitoring_custom_metrics revoked → soft-delete custom_script checks + custom_metric policies.

Reacts to the module's own MonitoringAlertFired event for the meta-alert path: monitoring's own dispatch failure rate. When notification failure rate for the tenant exceeds 25% over 15min: fire an internal MonitoringHealthCheckPersistentlyFailingNotification-equivalent for the ops team of the monitoring module itself.

P1 fail-over path. When PagerDuty's circuit opens: check whether the tenant has an active Opsgenie config; if yes: re-route pending P1 notifications to Opsgenie. When Opsgenie's circuit opens: mirror path via PagerDuty. Both open: page platform ops directly (bypasses tenant-side providers entirely).
